books.title: Dracula
books.creator: Bram Stoker
books.license: Public domain (text: Project Gutenberg #345 / audio: LibriVox)
books.description: Epistolary horror novel told through diaries, letters, and telegrams. Multi-narrator structure — Jonathan Harker, Mina Harker, Dr. Seward, Lucy Westenra, Van Helsing — makes it ideal for showcasing per-speaker visuals.

packs.name: Dracula — Vintage Cinema
packs.description: Atmospheric demo pack using only public-domain sources. F.W. Murnau's Nosferatu provides the visual grammar for vampires and Carpathian dread; Caspar David Friedrich landscapes set the gothic mood; period photographs portray the Victorian cast.

art.notes: Nosferatu (F. W. Murnau, 1922) film stills + 19th-century Romantic landscape painting + period portrait photography.

packs.license: Public Domain / CC0 — see assets-sources.md for per-image attribution.

books.title: Echo of Worlds
books.creator: M. R. Carey
books.license: Copyrighted — for personal use only. Not shipped with Storyplay.
books.description: Book two of the Pandominion. A trillion-world civilisation faces a hostile machine intelligence — Carey's interlocking POVs span ambassadors, spies, and a sentient rabbit.

packs.name: Echo of Worlds — Default
packs.description: Personal pack — characters, scenes, and key moments for M.R. Carey's Pandominion world. Empty until regenerated.

art.notes: Personal pack; original AI-generated images were lost in a tooling accident on 2026-05-12 and have not yet been regenerated.

packs.license: All-rights-reserved — personal pack, not for redistribution.

books.title: Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus
books.creator: Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley
books.license: Public domain (text: Project Gutenberg #84 / audio: LibriVox)
books.description: Gothic novel of creation and abandonment. Walton's framing letters bracket Victor Frankenstein's confession; the Creature's own long testimony forms the moral core. Rich speaking cast — perfect for showcasing per-character art.

packs.name: Frankenstein — Romantic Engravings
packs.description: Pairs Mary Shelley's text with Romantic-era painting and engraving. Holst's 1831 frontispiece for the Creature; Friedrich's Alpine landscapes for the Arctic and Mont Blanc; Blake's mythic figures for the Creature's philosophical monologues.

art.notes: Theodor von Holst's 1831 frontispiece, Caspar David Friedrich landscapes, William Blake plates, 19th-century anatomical engravings.

packs.license: Public Domain — see assets-sources.md for per-image attribution.

books.title: A Scandal in Bohemia
books.creator: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.license: Public domain (text: Project Gutenberg #1661 / audio: LibriVox)
books.description: The first story in *The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes*. Holmes is hired by the King of Bohemia to recover compromising letters from the only person ever to outwit him — Irene Adler. Short, dialogue-rich, ideal for showcasing the per-speaker visual system.

packs.name: Sherlock Holmes — Paget Originals
packs.description: Pairs Conan Doyle's text with Sidney Paget's original Strand Magazine illustrations — the canonical visual reference for Holmes, drawn for the actual scenes. The pack proves the system: public-domain art chosen by humans, no AI involved.

art.notes: Sidney Paget's original Strand Magazine illustrations, drawn by Doyle's contemporary specifically for these stories. Canonical reference for Holmes' visual identity (deerstalker, Inverness cape, hawk-nosed profile).

packs.license: Public Domain — Sidney Paget illustrations originally published in The Strand Magazine, 1891-1908.
